name: Guinea-Bissau

visa_free.notes: ECOWAS member state. Citizens enjoy free movement under the ECOWAS Protocol on Free Movement of Persons, Residence and Establishment (entered into force 30 April 1980) and supplementary protocols. ECOWAS nationals may also use a national identity card in lieu of a passport. Wikipedia's Guinea-Bissau visa policy page lists 14 ECOWAS members as visa-exempt for an 'indefinite period of stay' per the 1980 ECOWAS agreement; 90 days is the standard Phase I right under the protocol.

visa_on_arrival.notes: All non-exempt nationals may obtain a single-entry short-stay visa on arrival at Osvaldo Vieira International Airport. Requires a letter of authorisation from the Migration Office of Guinea-Bissau obtained in advance. Short-stay single-entry visa: 30 days (fee EUR 61.45 + EUR 2.50 processing per rgb-visa.com/Fees.html). Longer-duration and multiple-entry visas (3 months, 6 months, 1 year) are also available through the e-visa/pre-enrollment system (rgb-visa.com) or at a Guinea-Bissau embassy. US State Dept confirms: 'A visa is required. You can get it on arrival.'

e_visa.notes: Pre-enrollment e-visa available at https://www.rgb-visa.com/ — described as 'Republic of Guinea-Bissau Electronic Visa Application System', operated by CETIS. Applicant receives visa slip by email to print and present at border. This portal is NOT on an official Guinea-Bissau government domain (.gw) — source_official is false per strict domain rule. Fee schedule (from rgb-visa.com/Fees.html, all prices + EUR 2.50 processing fee): Short Stay single 30 days = EUR 61.45; Multiple 3 months = EUR 70.45; Transit/stopover single 1 day = EUR 55.00; Multiple 6 months = EUR 88.32; Multiple 1 year = EUR 100.00; Courtesy Visa single 1 month = EUR 61.45. The portal is referenced by third-party official sources as Guinea-Bissau's operational e-visa system.

visa_policy.notes: Guinea-Bissau's official government website (gov.gw) does not resolve (DNS failure) at time of research — no official immigration portal with a .gw domain was accessible. The rgb-visa.com domain operates the electronic visa pre-enrollment system but is NOT on a Guinea-Bissau government domain. ECOWAS nationals are visa-free (ordinary passports); all other nationalities require a visa (on arrival, via embassy, or via e-visa pre-enrollment). The standard short-stay single-entry visa on arrival is valid for 30 days; longer stays require a multiple-entry visa applied for in advance. US State Dept confirms visa on arrival is available at Osvaldo Vieira International Airport. Yellow fever vaccination certificate is mandatory for all arriving travellers.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic or service passports issued by CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) member states do not require a visa for an indefinite period of stay. Based on the multilateral CPLP 'Acordo sobre Supressão de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Especiais e de Serviço'. Ordinary passport holders from these countries are NOT covered by this rule (subject to normal visa requirements unless separately exempt as ECOWAS members). Note: an Africa Visa Openness 2025 report (visaopenness.org) claims Guinea-Bissau switched São Tomé and Príncipe from visa-on-arrival to visa-free status for ordinary passports in 2025, but this could not be confirmed from an official Guinea-Bissau government source and commercial sources (Savory & Partners) continue to show visa required — treated as unverified and listed in gaps.

Agreement on visa exemption for holders of diplomatic, official and service passports signed by Belarus Minister of Foreign Affairs Maxim Ryzhenkov and Guinea-Bissau State Secretary for International Cooperation Fatumata Jau on 7 May 2025. Agreement will enter into legal force after both parties fulfil necessary domestic ratification procedures — NOT YET IN FORCE as of signing date. Status as of June 2026 is unconfirmed — no subsequent ratification announcement found.

Guinea-Bissau signed a visa exemption agreement with Serbia for holders of diplomatic, official and special passports in April 2023. As of latest available information (Wikipedia, 2025), the agreement has not yet entered into force. No official government domain source confirmed.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Guinea-Bissau government source. The gov.gw domain does not resolve. No official announcement of any CBI program found. Third-party sources confirm no formal CBI program exists.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) Free Movement Protocol (entered into force 30 April 1980) — visa-free for 14 ECOWAS member nationals with ordinary passports

CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) Acordo sobre Supressão de Vistos em Passaportes Diplomáticos, Especiais e de Serviço — visa-free for diplomatic/service passport holders of Angola, Brazil, Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Portugal, São Tomé and Príncipe, Vietnam
CPLP Mobility Agreement (signed Luanda 2021) — facilitates residency procedures among CPLP member states; does NOT extend ordinary-passport visa-free entry to Guinea-Bissau
Belarus–Guinea-Bissau visa exemption agreement for diplomatic/official/service passport holders (signed 7 May 2025, pending entry into force)
Serbia–Guinea-Bissau visa exemption agreement for diplomatic/official/special passport holders (signed April 2023, pending entry into force)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Prior record grouped all 14 ECOWAS visa-free nationals as a single entry under 'ECOWAS member state nationals'. Expanded to individual ISO3-keyed entries for each of the 14 member states (Benin, Burkina Faso, Cabo Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Liberia, Mali, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo) as required by the schema's directed-graph design.

CORRECTED: Prior record listed visa_on_arrival max_stay_days as 90. Corrected to 30 days, which is the standard short-stay single-entry visa duration per rgb-visa.com/Fees.html fee schedule. Longer stays (3 months, 6 months, 1 year) require multiple-entry visas obtained in advance via e-visa or embassy. The 90-day figure is retained as context in notes.

CORRECTED: Prior record cited travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/Visa-Reciprocity-and-Civil-Documents-by-Country/Guinea-Bissau.html — that URL only covers US visa reciprocity schedule, not Guinea-Bissau entry requirements. Updated source_url for visa_on_arrival to the correct US State Dept country information page (International-Travel-Country-Information-Pages/Guinea-Bissau.html).

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: CPLP diplomatic/service passport visa suppression — covers Angola, Brazil, Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Portugal, São Tomé and Príncipe, Vietnam. Wikipedia confirms 'indefinite' stay; source remains non-official.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Belarus agreement (signed May 2025) not yet in force — source mfa.gov.by confirmed.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Serbia agreement (April 2023) not yet in force — Wikipedia confirmed.

NEW FINDING: Africa Visa Openness 2025 report (visaopenness.org) claims Guinea-Bissau changed São Tomé and Príncipe status from visa-on-arrival to visa-free for ordinary passports in 2025. This conflicts with Wikipedia and commercial aggregators (Savory & Partners) which show visa required. Cannot confirm from an official Guinea-Bissau source — classified as unverified gap.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: gov.gw still does not resolve. rgb-visa.com still active and still not on official .gw domain.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No CBI, RBI, or fast-track programs.
gaps: Guinea-Bissau's official government website (gov.gw) does not resolve — no official immigration portal accessible on a .gw domain.
No official list of visa-exempt or visa-on-arrival nationalities published on a Guinea-Bissau government domain was found.
rgb-visa.com appears to operate Guinea-Bissau's electronic visa system but is a private .com domain — data from it cannot be marked source_official.

São Tomé and Príncipe ordinary passport status: Africa Visa Openness 2025 report claims Guinea-Bissau switched São Tomé from visa-on-arrival to visa-free in 2025, but this cannot be confirmed from an official Guinea-Bissau government source and is contradicted by commercial aggregators. Listed here as unverified; ordinary São Tomé passport holders should verify current requirements with a Guinea-Bissau embassy.

CPLP Mobility Agreement (Luanda 2021): Guinea-Bissau ratification status for ordinary-passport travel facilitation not confirmed from an official Guinea-Bissau source. The Luanda Agreement primarily addresses residency, not visa-free tourism entry.
CPLP diplomatic/service passport visa suppression agreement: Guinea-Bissau's specific ratification date not confirmed from an official .gw domain source.
Serbia visa waiver agreement (April 2023): source is Wikipedia only; entry-into-force date unknown.
Belarus visa waiver agreement (7 May 2025): signed per official Belarus MFA (mfa.gov.by); ratification by Guinea-Bissau not confirmed; entry-into-force status as of June 2026 unknown.

Max stay for ECOWAS nationals: Wikipedia states 'indefinite period of stay' per the 1980 ECOWAS agreement, but the ECOWAS Phase I protocol standard right is 90 days. No Guinea-Bissau official source specifies the exact duration.

No conditional entry rule found granting visa-free or visa-on-arrival access based on the traveller holding a valid US, Schengen, UK, Canada, Australia, Japan, UAE or other third-country visa or residence permit.
No OCI or analogous special diaspora/heritage status conferring special entry rights to Guinea-Bissau was found.
No official RBI, golden visa, or investor residence program found.
No official fast-track, digital-nomad, or startup visa scheme found.

Timor-Leste and Equatorial Guinea (CPLP members): their diplomatic/service passport status in Guinea-Bissau was not confirmed — Wikipedia lists only 7 CPLP members (excluding Timor-Leste and Equatorial Guinea) as covered by the diplomatic/service passport suppression agreement.

outbound_official.note: no official Guinea-Bissau government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Guinea-Bissau's MFA website (mne.gw) is the only identified origin-side government portal; it has been returning HTTP 503 since at least mid-2024 (all Wayback Machine captures from August 2024 onward show 503). The last working archived snapshot (December 2023, via web.archive.org/web/20231203160447/https://mne.gw/) confirms the site was a diplomatic news portal with categories Agenda, Diplomacia, Noticias, Topico — no outbound visa-free destinations list or consular citizen-services section was present. No other official .gw domain page (gov.gw, presidencia.gw) hosts an outbound visa list for Guinean citizens.

sources_checked: http://www.gov.gw/ (DNS does not resolve — inaccessible, confirmed again during re-validation)

https://www.travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/international-travel/International-Travel-Country-Information-Pages/Guinea-Bissau.html (US State Dept country info page — confirms visa required, available on arrival)

https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/Visa-Reciprocity-and-Civil-Documents-by-Country/Guinea-Bissau.html (US State Dept visa reciprocity page — confirmed this page covers US visa reciprocity schedules only, NOT Guinea-Bissau entry requirements; prior record source corrected)

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Guinea-Bissau (aggregator — not marked source_official; confirms 14 ECOWAS members visa-free indefinitely, CPLP diplomatic/service passports only, Serbia not yet in force)
https://www.rgb-visa.com/ (private .com domain — not source_official; confirms e-visa system active, ECOWAS ID card accepted, biometric visa required for all others)
https://www.rgb-visa.com/Fees.html (private .com — confirms short-stay single-entry = 30 days EUR 61.45, multiple-entry options up to 1 year)
https://www.mfa.gov.by/en/press/news_mfa/a1da422d3a299a57.html (Belarus MFA official — confirms Belarus agreement signed 7 May 2025, pending ratification)

https://www.visaopenness.org/2025-visa-openness-remains-a-work-in-progress/regional-economic-communities/economic-community-of-west-african-states-ecowas/ (Africa Visa Openness / AfDB-affiliated — claims Guinea-Bissau switched São Tomé to visa-free in 2025; unverified gap)
https://www.savoryandpartners.com/blog/sao-tome-and-principe-visa-free-countries (commercial aggregator — lists Guinea-Bissau as requiring a traditional visa for São Tomé passport holders)
